title: Collection helpers use structural sharing for the reference-based dirty tracking
description: updateEntityById, replaceEntityById, and removeEntityById always returned a NEW array, even when no id matched or the element kept its reference. Because AggregateRoot.changedKeys is a reference-based per-key diff, a no-op helper call through setState({...state, items: updateEntityById(...)}) falsely marked the collection key dirty and made partial-write repositories write an untouched child table. Decision 2026-07-06 (maintainer challenged the earlier docs-only route on bead 4nmw): the helpers now practice structural sharing (Immer/Redux convention): return the ORIGINAL array when no element reference changed (no match, same-reference updater result, same-reference replacement, absent id on remove), a new array only on a real change with sibling identity preserved. BREAKING within unreleased 3.0.0: return type changed T[] -> ReadonlyArray<T>, because the result may BE the frozen input; returning T[] would be a mutation lie. Implemented on fix/audit-p3-cleanups; supersedes the docs-route resolution of ddd-kit-ts-4nmw.
acceptance_criteria: Tests pin: original reference returned for no-match, no-op update, same-reference replacement, and absent-id remove; new array with sibling identity preserved on real change; return type ReadonlyArray<T> (typecheck); JSDoc of all three helpers states the structural-sharing contract; CHANGELOG breaking bullet under 3.0.0. Full gates green: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.
notes: Architecture review 2026-07-06 (15-lens skill, 8 lenses in scope): design confirmed as best practice with strong cross-lens agreement. 12 PASS, 2 deliberate minor WARNs, 0 blockers. Bernhardt GB-01/04/15 (pure value transformation, mock-free output-based tests), Fowler MF-20 (structural sharing = the Immer/Redux immutable-update idiom; reference as honest change signal), Evans EE-05/EE-26 (ReadonlyArray return is literally the documented invariant-protection fix pattern), Wirfs-Brock RW-14 (contract now truthful), Martin RM-16 (CQS), Khorikov VK-06/08 (reference-identity assertions test the CONTRACT, not the implementation). Deliberately not addressed: silent-miss semantics (helper has no domain context; must-exist belongs to the aggregate method, which can now detect a miss via result === input; a throwing/Result variant would be MF-08 speculative generality) and the map allocation on the miss path (small collections, Gregg measure-first). Decision also recorded in docs/guide/design-decisions.md.
close_reason: Implemented on fix/audit-p3-cleanups (audit follow-up batch), hardened by a high-effort review round (six regressions found and fixed with RED tests); gates green: 1080/1080 tests, typecheck, build

title: Enforce absolute Value Object value semantics
description: Fix the final review findings: reject mutable or reference-compared built-ins from functional and class-based Value Objects, require real Promise/Error brands so Symbol.toStringTag spoofed plain data remains structural, narrow DomainStateMachine transition callbacks to their concrete source state, and cover all behavior with regression/type tests and docs.
close_reason: Enforced immutable value semantics for all admitted VO values; added regression tests, docs, and source-state typing fix.

title: Reject unsafe custom class cloning in value objects
description: Make vo()/ValueObject fail closed for custom class instances and unknown built-ins that cannot be reconstructed generically. Cover public, private-field, non-enumerable-state classes, boxed Symbol, and preserve cross-realm plain objects.
close_reason: Rejected unsafe custom class and built-in subclass cloning in vo()/ValueObject; added adversarial, cross-realm, and null-prototype coverage; updated docs.

title: Generalize masked built-in classification for value objects
description: Prevent data Symbol.toStringTag overrides from turning RegExp, buffers, boxed primitives, Error, Promise, WeakMap, and WeakSet into invalid vo() pseudo-instances. Add own/inherited descriptor regression tests and fail closed for forbidden built-ins.
close_reason: Generalized accessor-safe built-in classification across supported atomic types, rejected masked Promise/Weak collections, added BigInt value semantics, and covered own/inherited tags plus cross-realm behavior; all release gates pass.

title: Handle data toStringTag overrides on mutable built-ins
description: Fix Date, Map, and Set classification when own or inherited data Symbol.toStringTag descriptors mask their visible tag. Preserve deepFreeze mutator blocking and vo() built-in cloning, with regression tests.
close_reason: Added descriptor-aware internal-slot fallback for Date, Map, and Set; deepFreeze and vo now handle own/inherited data toStringTag overrides with regression coverage and all release gates passing.

title: Preserve deepFreeze mutator blocking with toStringTag accessors
description: Fix the reviewed regression where genuine Date, Map, and Set instances with Symbol.toStringTag accessors bypass internal-slot mutator shadowing. Add regression tests for own and inherited accessors and run release gates.
close_reason: Fixed internal-slot mutator blocking for Date, Map, and Set with own or inherited toStringTag accessors; added regression coverage and passed release gates.

title: Close inherited accessor and prepared snapshot gaps
description: Prevent inherited Symbol.toStringTag accessors from executing in domain machine data/deepFreeze, and eliminate redundant context cloning in the stateful wrapper's prepared path with regression coverage.
close_reason: Rejected inherited toStringTag accessors without invocation across realms and arrays, made deepFreeze tag detection accessor-safe, and eliminated redundant wrapper context cloning with deterministic identity tests; all release/docs gates pass.

title: Fix final DomainStateMachine review findings
description: Prevent Symbol.toStringTag accessors from executing during data validation, align public types with deep runtime immutability, and remove redundant wrapper definition/result copies with regression and type tests.
close_reason: Prevented toStringTag accessor execution, added recursive readonly API contracts and type tests, and reused prepared wrapper definitions/results; all release and docs gates pass.

title: Close remaining DomainStateMachine contract gaps
description: Fix review findings: preserve non-enumerable transition fields, add snapshot invariant validation across initialization/reconstitution/transitions, document pure callback requirements, and accept cross-realm plain data without accepting custom classes.
close_reason: Closed snapshot invariant, definition-copy, cross-realm data, documentation, and regression-test gaps

title: Harden DomainStateMachine runtime contracts
description: Fix review findings: reject async/malformed reducer results and unknown definition keys, prevent mutable-definition TOCTOU and wrapper reentrancy, enforce truly immutable plain machine data, reject Array subclasses, and add regression tests/docs.
close_reason: Fixed all review findings with regression coverage; full tests, typecheck, build, prepublish, and docs build pass.

title: Harden domain state machine guard and data contracts
description: Fix review findings: guards must return strict booleans, terminal states must not declare outgoing transitions, and machine data must reject custom class instances that cannot be safely cloned without breaking private brands.
close_reason: Strict guard result validation, terminal definition validation, plain-data rejection for custom class instances, tests, and docs added.

title: Harden DomainStateMachine runtime edge cases
description: Fix second review findings: preserve explicit null/undefined context updates, preserve own __proto__ transition/state keys through wrapper definition copies, and report malformed runtime definitions as InvalidDomainMachineDefinitionError instead of raw TypeError.
close_reason: Preserved explicit null/undefined context updates, copied definitions through null-prototype maps so own __proto__ keys survive, and validated malformed runtime definitions as structured errors. Covered by regression tests.

title: Fix inherited event-name transition lookup in DomainStateMachine
description: Runtime events with prototype property names such as toString or constructor are treated as valid transitions because getTransition indexes the on-map without an own-property check. Add regression tests and require own transition keys.
close_reason: Fixed DomainStateMachine transition lookup to require own transition keys and added regression coverage for Object.prototype event names.

title: createDomainEvent returns unfrozen events — mutating subscriber poisons later handlers
description:
Architecture review finding (EE-18 / GH-04 / GY-04 — Evans, Hohpe, Young).

src/aggregate/domain-event.ts:200 — createDomainEvent returns a plain object literal; nothing is frozen. EventBusImpl.publish (src/events/event-bus.ts:131) passes the SAME event reference to every subscribed handler via handlersForType.slice().map(h => h(event)). A subscriber that writes to event.payload.* mutates the object that subsequent subscribers will see. In an event-sourced flow the same risk applies to the historical event references stored in _pendingEvents.

Events are a foundational immutable concept in DDD — this contract leak undermines EE-18 (events describe past facts), GH-04 (messages are immutable), and GY-04 (event store is append-only / events never edited).

Fix: deepFreeze (or at minimum Object.freeze) the event before returning from createDomainEvent. Consider also freezing on push into _pendingEvents/_domainEvents so historical refs are immutable. Verify test coverage includes a 'mutating subscriber does not affect peer subscribers' case.
close_reason: createDomainEvent now deepFreezes before returning. Events are immutable across the EventBus handler chain — mutating subscribers throw and cannot poison subsequent handlers

title: IAggregateRoot is missing markPersisted in its public interface
description:
Architecture review finding (RW-04, Wirfs-Brock).

src/aggregate/aggregate-root.ts:35 — IAggregateRoot declares only id and version. IRepository.save (src/repo/repository.ts:46) is documented to call aggregate.markPersisted(newVersion) after a successful write, but markPersisted lives only on the concrete AggregateRoot and EventSourcedAggregate classes. A consumer who implements the published IAggregateRoot interface directly (legitimate per the type) silently breaks the repository contract — no compile error, but the in-memory version never reflects the persisted version and pending events never clear.

Fix: add markPersisted(version: Version): void to IAggregateRoot. Alternatively split out IPersistableAggregateRoot extends IAggregateRoot and update IRepository<TAgg> to require TAgg extends IPersistableAggregateRoot. Either way the JSDoc on IRepository.save should match the actual interface surface.
close_reason: markPersisted promoted from concrete class to IAggregateRoot interface. Repository implementers can now code against the interface and call markPersisted via the contract

title: AggregateRoot.commit() defaults autoVersionBump to false — breaks OCC silently
description:
Architecture review finding (VV-25, Vernon).

src/aggregate/aggregate-root.ts:217 — AggregateConfig.autoVersionBump defaults to false on AggregateRoot. Calling commit(newState, event) without the explicit bumpVersion=true argument applies state and records an event but does NOT increment the aggregate version. Any IRepository implementation that compares aggregate.version to the stored version will then miss concurrent writes — optimistic concurrency silently disabled.

EventSourcedAggregate sensibly defaults autoVersionBump to true; AggregateRoot should mirror that. The Vernon (IDDD) axiom is: state change == version bump.

Fix: flip the default of AggregateConfig.autoVersionBump to true for AggregateRoot. Update tests and docs (the JSDoc on AggregateConfig.autoVersionBump explicitly defends the current default — revise it). Verify the commit() and setState() call paths still behave correctly for callers that opt out via { autoVersionBump: false }.
close_reason: commit() now always bumps the version (bumpVersion parameter removed). Recording an event implies version-worthy change; un-coupled setState path still honors autoVersionBump for users who need state-only mutations

title: Contract suites: replace legacy-alias matching with a pre-v3 copy diagnostic
description: The StructuredError migration added legacy PascalCase aliases to the contract matchers (chainContainsErrorNamedAnyOf with [CODE, LegacyName]) so v2 kit copies keep matching. Decision 2026-07-05: that silently certifies adapters running against the wrong kit copy (a mixed-major dependency graph the v3 suite cannot validate anyway) and reintroduces a shadow identity for errors. Replace with codes-only matching plus a diagnostic: when the code is absent but the pre-v3 PascalCase name IS found in the cause chain, fail with a targeted message naming the old kit copy as the cause. Update contract-assertions JSDoc and LLM.md accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests: matching code passes; missing code fails with the plain contract message; a chain carrying the legacy PascalCase error fails with a diagnostic naming the pre-v3 copy. All contract suites re-keyed to the helper; no legacy alias remains as an accepted match; docs claims updated. Full gates green.
close_reason: Implemented version-neutrally: codes are the only accepted identity, no pre-v3 knowledge in code; describeError renders cause-chain names generically; interpretation moved to the CHANGELOG troubleshooting note; gates green

title: Migrate kit errors to StructuredError with codes (decided: yes)
description: Design decision, needs the maintainer. base-error 8 positions StructuredError (code/category/retryable/details) as the technical error to throw; matchError dispatches exhaustively on code and the public-error catalog resolves primarily by code. Kit errors are BaseError subclasses with pinned NAMES but no CODES, so matchError, defineErrorClassSet, and code resolution do not engage with kit errors. Codes and names are not mutually exclusive: kit errors could become StructuredErrors carrying both. Breaking for consumers matching on class shape; 3.0.0 (unreleased) is the last painless window. If declined, close this bead with the rationale.
acceptance_criteria: Kit concrete errors extend StructuredError with stable SCREAMING_SNAKE codes and hierarchy-derived categories; DomainError/InfrastructureError stay consumer-ergonomic (code is the only new required input; category/retryable defaulted); names unchanged and pinned; matchError works exhaustively over kit codes (test); retryable preserved on ConcurrencyConflictError via the structured field (retry classifier test stays green); code-vs-name convention documented in errors.ts JSDoc and the error guide; CHANGELOG breaking entry under 3.0.0; api-surface unchanged or additions pinned. Full gates green: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.
notes: Decision 2026-07-05 by maintainer: YES (UNBEDINGT), Option A confirmed: kit errors become real StructuredError subclasses, accepting base-error's name === code model (one identifier, SCREAMING_SNAKE; the old PascalCase names are gone, 3.0.0 window). Claude's conditions hold: consumer bases keep code+message as the only obligations (category/retryable defaulted). ADDITIONAL BINDING CONSTRAINT from maintainer: consumers must be able to use the kit properly WITHOUT adopting base-error; base-error is an on-top benefit, never a prerequisite. Concretely: instanceof checks go through kit-exported bases, branching works via plain switch on error.code (plain field), retryable/cause are plain properties, DomainError subclassing is kit API; matchError/catalogs/toProblem are optional extras. Docs must show the no-base-error path first; a dedicated test pins the kit-imports-only flow. defineErrors as primary definition form was vetoed (factories lose hierarchy, typed fields, constructor messages; catalog not extensible); extensibility for consumers comes from definePublicErrors (tq3e) plus exported KitErrorCode union.
close_reason: Implemented: all 27 kit errors are StructuredErrors (code = name, SCREAMING_SNAKE), categories DOMAIN/INFRASTRUCTURE/WIRING via shared KitWiringError base, consumer bases keep code+message-only ergonomics, no-base-error path pinned by test, contract matchers re-keyed with legacy aliases, validation gate hardened by category, docs/examples/LLM.md migrated; review round applied; gates green

title: Replace the setState bumpVersion flag argument with two named methods
description: AggregateRoot.setState(next, bumpVersion) is a Clean Code flag argument: true/false at call sites is unreadable and the OCC decision (loss under concurrent write acceptable?) deserves a word, not a boolean. Decision 2026-07-05: split into setState(next) (bumps the version, the safe default) and setStateWithoutVersionBump(next) (loud name for the rare, dangerous opt-out), applied consistently across the API, JSDoc, tests, docs, examples, and the 3.0.0 upgrade checklist (3.0.0 is unreleased, so the two-argument signature never ships). Also removes the bivariant-override footgun of the flag signature.
acceptance_criteria: Compile-time and runtime tests pin the new contract: setState(next) bumps exactly once per commit cycle as before, setStateWithoutVersionBump(next) preserves the no-bump semantics, the old two-argument call is a compile error. All call sites, JSDoc, CHANGELOG upgrade checklist, docs/ guides, examples/, README and LLM.md use the new methods; grep for bumpVersion finds no stale references outside the changelog history. Full gates green: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.
notes: Implemented on refactor/setstate-named-methods. Additionally swept the whole library for further flag-argument violations per user request. Found and fixed: EventSourcedAggregate.apply(event, isNew = true) (protected API, flag removed entirely; apply is only for new facts, replay has its own entry points), private dispatchAndCommit(event, isNew) (split into record-free dispatch plus apply layering record+bump), private assertReplayTargetFresh(checkUnpersistedVersion) (split into assertReplayTargetHasNoPendingEvents and assertReplayTargetHasNoUnpersistedVersion). Explicitly NOT violations: contract-assertions assert(condition: boolean) (the boolean IS the data) and options-object booleans like deepFreezeState (named at the call site). No boolean parameters or boolean-literal call sites remain in src outside tests.
close_reason: Implemented: setState always bumps, setStateWithoutVersionBump for the loud opt-out; apply isNew flag removed; private flag helpers split into named guards; docs, examples, changelog migrated; gates green

title: toPublicErrorView is not total and duck-typed publicIssues bypasses the whitelist
description: src/presentation/public-error-view.ts:89-96 and 118-126. error.publicIssues() is called unguarded: if it throws, the presenter throws inside the 500 path, breaking the documented totality over unknown. Any non-kit object with a publicIssues() method also passes the duck-type check, so its raw name is emitted as the client-facing code and its arbitrary return value ships to the client in details.issues (verified leak of internal infrastructure detail). Fix: wrap the publicIssues() call in try/catch with fallback to the INTERNAL_ERROR view, validate the result is an array, and only use name as code when it matches a known kit pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Regression tests in public-error-view.test.ts: 1) an error whose publicIssues() throws yields the INTERNAL_ERROR fallback view instead of throwing; 2) a non-kit object exposing publicIssues() does not leak its raw name as code nor its return value into details.issues; 3) a publicIssues() returning a non-array is not attached. Tests must fail before the fix. Full gates green: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/public-error-view-totality: outer try/catch totality, SCREAMING_SNAKE capability gate, PublicIssue whitelist sanitization; tests and gates green

title: Prototype pollution in shallowCopyOwned via own __proto__ state key
description: src/entity/entity.ts:294-300. The plain-object branch copies state with Object.assign(Object.create(proto), value). Object.assign uses [[Set]], so an own enumerable __proto__ data key on the input (typical of JSON.parse rows fed to reconstitute factories) invokes the Object.prototype.__proto__ setter: the copy gets an attacker-controlled prototype and the key is dropped. Runs on every Entity construction and setState. Fix: copy own enumerable keys with Object.defineProperty, same pattern as mergeMetadata in domain-event.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Regression tests in entity.test.ts: constructor, setState, null-prototype, and array states carrying an own __proto__ data key throw the new HostileStateKeyError (setState keeps the previous state); a benign null-prototype state round-trips with its prototype preserved. Tests failed before the fix. HostileStateKeyError is exported from the main entry and pinned in api-surface.test.ts. Full gates green: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.
notes: Design decision 2026-07-05: LOUD REJECTION instead of preserving the key as data. Preserving would re-arm pollution in downstream [[Set]]-based consumers of entity.state (workflow-review finding); silent dropping would hide attacks and mutate data. Implemented via assertNoHostileKey in shallowCopyOwned plus the named HostileStateKeyError (crash-loud posture, not a DomainError). The copy itself also moved from Object.assign to object spread (CreateDataProperty) as defense in depth. Follow-up for mergeMetadata tracked separately.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/entity-proto-pollution: loud rejection via HostileStateKeyError, spread-based copy, tests and gates green

title: fix(handler): per-commit aggregateVersion stamp is unsafe as an idempotency watermark
description:
Why: withCommit stamps ALL pending events of one aggregate with the FINAL commit version (all events of a multi-event commit share it), while the DomainEvent.aggregateVersion JSDoc explicitly recommends the field for "idempotency watermarks". A projector using position <= watermark skip logic that crashes between two same-version events skips the second one on restart: silent projection loss.

Fix proposal (two steps):
1. Docs fix now (non-breaking): correct the JSDoc in src/aggregate/domain-event.ts and the outbox guide: aggregateVersion is for ordering across commits and debugging; idempotency MUST use eventId dedup (GH-11 style processed-id set), not a version cursor. Also adjust the contract-suite assertion message that presents the commit-version stamp as watermark-ready.
2. Evaluate for v3: stamp a per-event monotonic sequence in the harvest envelope. Note that per-event unique versions do NOT exist in the state-stored model (commit(newState, [e1, e2]) bumps the version once for two events), so the honest design is (aggregateVersion, commitSequenceIndex) rather than fake per-event versions. For EventSourcedAggregate a true per-event stream position (startVersion + i) exists and could be stamped correctly.
acceptance_criteria: JSDoc and guides no longer recommend aggregateVersion as an idempotency watermark; eventId dedup documented as the idempotency mechanism; follow-up decision recorded for the per-event sequence design.
close_reason: JSDoc guidance in domain-event.ts and handler.ts aligned with the outbox guide (eventId dedup, per-commit watermark semantics); v3 per-event sequence evaluation split into a follow-up bead

title: fix(es-aggregate): restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents does not validate the restored snapshot state
description:
Why: EventSourcedAggregate.restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents (src/aggregate/event-sourced-aggregate.ts:206) assigns this._state = freezeShallow(this.fromSnapshotState(snapshot.state)) without calling validateState. With zero events after the snapshot, NO validation runs at all. AggregateRoot.restoreFromSnapshot does validate: the two restore paths are inconsistent, and a corrupt snapshot store silently yields invalid aggregates (Vernon VV-29: reconstitution preserves invariants).

Fix: call this.validateState(restored) before assigning the restored state; catch DomainError and return err(e) with rollback to the previous state and version, consistent with the existing replay contract of this method (all-or-nothing). Non-DomainError throws keep propagating.

Tests: corrupt snapshot state returns Err and leaves the aggregate untouched (state AND version); valid snapshot behavior unchanged; zero-events-after-snapshot case validates too.
acceptance_criteria: validateState runs on every restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents call; DomainError surfaces as Err with full rollback; tests cover the corrupt-snapshot and zero-events cases.
close_reason: Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean

title: fix(events): createDomainEvent deep-freezes caller-owned payload and metadata in place
description:
Why: createDomainEvent (src/aggregate/domain-event.ts:437) deep-freezes the assembled event WITHOUT cloning payload or options.metadata first, so the caller-owned object graphs are frozen in place (reproduced against dist: payload nested objects and a reused metadata object become frozen and later mutation throws TypeError far from the cause). This contradicts the vo() contract ("never freezes the caller own object graph") and is documented nowhere. Typical trigger: this.recordEvent("X", { items: this.state.items }) freezes parts of live aggregate state.

Fix options:
(a) RECOMMENDED: clone-then-freeze. Deep-clone payload and metadata before the freeze (structuredClone, or a cloneForVo-style walk). Note: structuredClone throws on function-bearing payloads; that is arguably correct (events are plain data by contract) but is a behavior change to call out in the CHANGELOG. Symbol keys are dropped by structuredClone; document that events carry string-keyed plain data.
(b) Fallback: keep in-place freeze but document the ownership transfer loudly in the JSDoc and docs/guide/domain-events.md.

Regression tests: caller payload object stays mutable after createDomainEvent; a metadata object reused across two events stays extensible; event itself remains deeply frozen.
acceptance_criteria: Caller-owned payload/metadata are never frozen by createDomainEvent (or the transfer is prominently documented if option b is chosen); tests pin the chosen behavior; CHANGELOG updated.
close_reason: Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean

title: fix(presentation): toPublicErrorView throws TypeError on null/undefined input
description:
Why: toPublicErrorView documents "Total over unknown: degrades to a generic INTERNAL_ERROR view rather than throwing", but hasPublicIssues (src/presentation/public-error-view.ts:114) reads error.publicIssues without a null guard. toPublicErrorView(null) and toPublicErrorView(undefined) throw TypeError (reproduced against dist). A caught "throw null" or reject(undefined) then crashes the error presenter itself in the 500 path.

Fix: add a null/object guard in hasPublicIssues: return typeof error === "object" && error !== null && typeof (error as { publicIssues?: unknown }).publicIssues === "function". Add regression tests for null, undefined, string and number inputs asserting the INTERNAL_ERROR fallback view. CHANGELOG entry under Unreleased/Fixed.
acceptance_criteria: toPublicErrorView(null) and (undefined) return the INTERNAL_ERROR view; tests cover null/undefined/primitive inputs; no behavior change for Error inputs.
close_reason: Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean

title: vo()/ValueObject: global or sticky RegExp becomes unusable (lastIndex frozen)
description: cloneForVo admits RegExp as an atomic built-in, then deepFreeze Object.freezes it, freezing the own lastIndex property. A global (g) or sticky (y) regex writes lastIndex on every .test()/.exec(), so vo({re:/a/g}).re.test('x') throws TypeError: Cannot assign to read only property 'lastIndex'. Decision (DDD value-object semantics): a global/sticky regex carries observable mutable scan state (lastIndex), so it is not a value. Reject it at construction with a clear message; keep non-global regex (genuinely immutable + side-effect-free, analogous to the neutralized Date/Map/Set). Found via code-review workflow.
acceptance_criteria: vo()/ValueObject reject a global or sticky RegExp with a descriptive TypeError; a non-global RegExp is still admitted and usable (.test()/.exec() work); regression tests cover both.
close_reason: Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 900 tests + build green

title: deepOmit applies key filtering to array indices, corrupting arrays and voEqualsExcept
description: deep-omit.ts array branch (line 129) applies shouldIgnoreKey to every own array key including numeric index keys. A predicate matching an index string, e.g. ignoreKeyPredicate: k => k === '2', silently drops that element, leaving a hole; via deepEqualExcept/voEqualsExcept two arrays that differ at that index compare equal. Contradicts the documented contract ('Only applies to object properties'). Fix: never apply key filtering to numeric array-index keys; still allow filtering custom (non-index) array properties. Found via code-review workflow.
acceptance_criteria: deepOmit never drops array elements via ignoreKeys/ignoreKeyPredicate; custom non-index array props can still be filtered; voEqualsExcept reports arrays differing at an index as not equal; regression test covers it.
close_reason: Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 900 tests + build green

title: DomainStateMachine constructor mishandles explicit undefined snapshot argument
description:
The DomainStateMachine constructor (src/domain-state-machine/domain-state-machine.ts:87-99) distinguishes its two overloads by rest-parameter arity (snapshotInput.length === 1) instead of inspecting the actual value.

Calling `new DomainStateMachine(definition, undefined)` has arguments.length 2, so the rest param has length 1 even though no snapshot was actually supplied. This routes into the "snapshot supplied" branch, calling validateDomainMachineSnapshot(this.definition, undefined), which throws InvalidDomainMachineSnapshotError ("Domain machine snapshot must be an object") instead of falling back to the initial-snapshot path.

This breaks any caller that passes through an optionally-loaded snapshot directly, e.g. `new DomainStateMachine(def, await repo.loadSnapshot(id))` where loadSnapshot returns `Snapshot | undefined` (Map.get, a nullable DB column via `??`, etc).

Found via the code-review workflow (high effort, scoped to src/domain-state-machine) on branch codex/domain-state-machine.
acceptance_criteria: new DomainStateMachine(definition, undefined) behaves identically to new DomainStateMachine(definition) (constructs from definition.initial / initialContext()), verified by a test. Overload resolution no longer depends on arguments.length / rest-param arity.
close_reason: Fixed: constructor now resolves the overload by value, treating explicit undefined snapshot as no-snapshot (falls back to initial). Widened reconstitution overload to accept undefined. Added regression test + inverted the stale type-level assertion.

title: Run final DomainStateMachine DDD release audit
description: Perform the final integration review after the DDD contract work: public naming, error semantics, invariant evaluation, plain-data security boundaries, examples, migration guidance, changelog, API docs, and package exports.
acceptance_criteria: All child features are integrated without compatibility leftovers; full tests, typecheck, lint, package build, Typedoc, and VitePress build pass; architecture review has no unresolved major findings; public docs explain why this machine exists alongside XState; release notes and examples match actual behavior.
close_reason: Final release audit completed: no unresolved major architecture findings; 881 tests, lint, typecheck, package build, Typedoc, VitePress, package contents, runtime exports, declarations, naming, docs, and release notes verified.

title: Support typed domain transition rejections
description: Design and implement a guard decision contract that can carry a concrete DomainError without turning can() into an exception-based query. can() must return false for a domain rejection; dispatch() must throw the concrete typed DomainError; malformed guard results remain programming errors.
acceptance_criteria: Typed rejection API is documented and type-safe; can() returns false without throwing the rejection; dispatch() throws the exact DomainError instance/type; generic boolean rejection remains intentionally specified; callback exceptions and malformed values preserve atomic snapshot behavior; edge cases and immutable-data boundaries are tested.
close_reason: Added typed DomainError guard rejections with can/dispatch split semantics, exact error propagation, malformed-result validation, atomicity tests, docs, changelog, and generated API verification.

title: Add state-local machine invariants
description: Allow invariants to be declared next to the state whose context they constrain, while preserving or deliberately evolving the existing machine-level validateSnapshot contract. Apply invariants during initial creation, reconstitution, functional input preparation, and before committing transition results.
acceptance_criteria: State-local invariant syntax is typed by state/context; every snapshot entry path evaluates the correct invariant exactly once; failures remain structured snapshot/definition errors; terminal and self-transition cases are covered; documentation explains local versus cross-state invariants.
close_reason: Added typed state-local context invariants with strict runtime validation, documentation, and edge-case coverage.

title: Rename DomainMachineEvent to DomainMachineInput
description: Rename the pre-release public input contract across implementation, tests, examples, changelog, guide, and generated API source documentation. Clarify that an input may represent a command, observed fact, or internal trigger and is not automatically a published Domain Event.
acceptance_criteria: No public DomainMachineEvent symbol remains; all callbacks and generics use DomainMachineInput consistently; Domain Event terminology remains reserved for aggregate recordEvent/commit; typecheck, full tests, build, and docs build pass.
close_reason: Renamed the complete public and internal machine input contract, errors, callbacks, tests, examples, changelog, and guide; all tests, types, builds, and generated docs pass.

title: Strengthen DomainStateMachine DDD contracts
description: Evolve DomainStateMachine as a deliberately narrow DDD transition engine rather than a smaller XState. Scope: (1) rename DomainMachineEvent to DomainMachineInput before first release to distinguish machine inputs from published Domain Events; (2) support typed domain rejection reasons so can() returns false while dispatch() throws the concrete DomainError; (3) support state-local snapshot/context invariants while preserving reconstitution validation; (4) evaluate a definition analyzer for unreachable states, structural dead ends, and reviewable transition matrices; (5) document snapshot evolution/migrations, duplicate-message/idempotency patterns, and time as explicit domain input. Non-goals: nested/parallel states, timers, async reducers, entry/exit actions, actors/services, automatic EventBus dispatch, persistence, retries, or automatic recordEvent integration. Acceptance: public semantics remain deterministic and synchronous; outputs remain plain requested-work values; Domain Events continue through aggregate recordEvent/commit; all new contracts have edge-case tests, migration documentation, and release-ready API docs.
close_reason: Completed all DomainStateMachine DDD contract improvements, analyzer, production guidance, tests, and final release audit.

title: Complete domain state machine documentation
description: Make the saga example use the real DomainStateMachine API, add persistence/reconstitution documentation, and regenerate API docs after the module split.
close_reason: Completed the state machine guide, converted the executable saga example to the real machine API, added API TSDoc, and verified generated API and VitePress builds.

title: Split domain state machine into focused modules
description: Refactor the 1302-line domain-state-machine implementation into focused internal modules while preserving the public API and runtime behavior. Add an architecture regression test and run all quality gates.
close_reason: Split the domain state machine into focused internal modules, preserved the public API, and verified all tests, types, lint, and build.

title: Cover exact domain machine traversal limits
description: Add acceptance tests at exactly 256 levels, 10,000 unique object nodes, and 100,000 own properties. Verify via temporary >= mutation that the tests catch off-by-one regressions.
close_reason: Added exact-maximum acceptance tests for depth, unique-node, and own-property budgets; temporary >= mutation proved all three tests detect off-by-one regressions.

title: Enforce machine depth before shared-reference cache
description: The machine clone returns a shallow-previsited object from the seen cache before checking depth, so a depth-257 graph is accepted depending on property order. Move the depth check before memoization and add a regression.
close_reason: Depth is now validated before memoized shared references are resolved; property-order bypass covered by regression test.

title: Document Proxy trust boundary
description: Portable ECMAScript cannot reliably detect direct Proxy data without invoking traps. Narrow the DomainStateMachine and VO plain-data contracts explicitly: inputs must be trusted and Proxy-free; the helpers are not security/sandbox boundaries.
close_reason: Documented the portable Proxy-free trusted-input precondition for DomainStateMachine and Value Objects without claiming an impossible ECMAScript sandbox guarantee.

title: Bound domain machine data traversal
description: Add deterministic depth, node, and property limits to context/event/output cloning so malformed inputs cannot exhaust the JS stack or cause unbounded clone work. Cover all public boundaries.
close_reason: Added deterministic depth, unique-node, and own-property budgets across context/event/output cloning with regression coverage.

title: Preserve array own-property semantics in deepOmit
description: deepEqual now compares all array own properties and distinguishes sparse holes, but deepOmit still loops 0..length, fills holes, and drops symbols/custom properties. Consequently voEqualsExcept/deepEqualExcept can report arrays equal after unrelated exclusions even when symbol metadata or hole presence differs. Clone/filter arrays through own data descriptors and add symbol, custom-key, sparse, and ignore-symbol regressions.
close_reason: Preserved sparse arrays and all filtered own properties in deepOmit; covered deepEqualExcept regressions.

title: Reject Proxy-spoofed intrinsic constructors
description: isIntrinsicConstructorPrototype accepts any constructor whose Function#toString contains [native code]. A Proxy around a user function produces that string, so custom prototypes are accepted by vo() and DomainStateMachine and silently normalized. Compare the full intrinsic source against the expected local native constructor (cross-Realm compatible), add Object/Array proxy tests for VO and machine data, and document the validation boundary.
close_reason: Replaced native-code substring detection with captured intrinsic source verification; covered VO and state-machine Proxy spoofing without trap execution.

title: Preserve VO equality for object-valued Map keys and Set members
description: vo() clones Map keys and Set members, while deepEqual compares Map keys and Set membership by identity. Two VOs created from the same source with an object key/member therefore fail voEquals. Reject object keys/members or define and implement coherent structural collection equality; add regressions for same-source and structurally-equal inputs.
close_reason: Value equality is now fail-closed: object Map keys and Set members are rejected with explicit errors and regression coverage.

title: Make vo cloning descriptor-safe and lossless
description: cloneForVo invokes enumerable getters on plain objects/array indices and drops array symbol/custom properties plus non-enumerable symbol keys, despite the documented no-side-effect/symbol-preservation contract and deepEqual considering all symbol keys. Clone from data descriptors without property reads, reject accessors, and preserve the property set used by equality.
close_reason: VO cloning now uses data descriptors, rejects accessors without invocation, preserves array/symbol/sparse structure, compares array own properties, and bypasses overridden collection iterators.

title: Reject spoofed intrinsic prototypes in DomainStateMachine data
description: The state machine's hasIntrinsicConstructorPrototype checks only constructor name/prototype linkage. A user function named Object whose prototype parent is null passes as cross-realm Object.prototype, so custom-prototype context is accepted and normalized, silently dropping prototype behavior. Reuse/harden native intrinsic detection and add adversarial context/event/output tests.
close_reason: DomainStateMachine now uses native intrinsic constructor verification for cross-Realm Object/Array prototypes with adversarial context/event/output tests.

title: Make docs scripts package-manager independent
description: npm run docs:build invokes pnpm recursively and fails under enforced ignored-build policies before TypeDoc starts. Invoke local TypeDoc/VitePress binaries through the npm script PATH instead.
close_reason: Docs scripts now invoke local TypeDoc and VitePress binaries directly; npm run docs:build passes without nested pnpm policy checks.

title: Reject custom properties on state machine built-ins
description: Fix review finding: allowed built-ins such as Date, RegExp, Map, and Set must be pure intrinsic values. Reject built-in subclasses and own custom/accessor properties consistently before cloning so machine data cannot silently drop behavior or metadata. Error objects and boxed primitives remain unsupported domain-machine data.
close_reason: Rejected non-pure built-ins in domain state machine data and covered edge cases

title: Harden state machine descriptor boundaries consistently
description: Fix review findings: reject array accessor data without invoking getters, reject definition wrapper accessors with structured errors, and reject Map/Set subclasses so the documented data-only boundary is enforced consistently.
close_reason: Rejected descriptor accessors across definitions and array data without invoking getters; rejected Map/Set subclasses; added regression tests.

title: Reject state machine wrapper accessors
description: Fix review findings: runtime snapshots and reducer transition-result wrapper objects must be validated via property descriptors so accessors cannot execute arbitrary code or leak unstructured errors before structured domain-machine errors are raised.
close_reason: Rejected accessor runtime snapshots and transition result wrappers via descriptor validation; added regression tests.

title: Fix domain state machine transition error precedence
description: Forbidden or terminal transitions currently validate and clone full event payloads before transition lookup, so invalid payload data can mask can=false or InvalidDomainTransitionError. Preserve transition semantics while still rejecting invalid event data for existing transitions before guard/reducer execution.
close_reason: Fixed transition lookup to preserve forbidden-transition semantics before event payload cloning; covered missing and terminal transitions.

title: Harden domain state machine event immutability
description: Fix review findings: copy and deep-freeze domain machine events before guards/reducers; reject invalid event/output data with structured errors and tests.
close_reason: Copied and deep-froze events before guards/reducers, rejected invalid event data, added output invalid-data coverage, and updated docs.

title: Harden domain state machine data-only boundaries
description: Fix review findings: reject accessor properties in machine contexts and deep-copy/deep-freeze transition outputs so effect values cannot leak mutable references.
close_reason: Rejected accessor context data, deep-copied/deep-froze transition outputs, added tests, and documented the data-only boundary.

title: Harden domain state machine pure function isolation
description: Fix review findings: pure transition helpers must not let guards or reducers mutate caller-owned snapshots; add edge-case tests for context cloning semantics; clarify docs.
close_reason: Fixed pure function snapshot isolation, hardened context clone contract, added edge-case coverage, and updated docs.

title: Harden domain state machine context ownership
description: Fix production-readiness review findings: prevent mutable context leaks, align constructor snapshot typing with runtime validation, and document all state machine structured errors.
close_reason: Copied and deep-froze state machine contexts, aligned constructor snapshot overloads, and documented all structured errors

title: Harden domain state machine public API edge cases
description: Fix review findings for malformed snapshots, malformed events, invalid definition member shapes, and invalid reducer result outputs in the domain state machine public API.
close_reason: Fixed malformed input and reducer result edge cases with tests

title: Clarify DomainStateMachine outputs are not emitted events
description: Make the Domain State Machine guide explicit that transition outputs are plain returned values, not emitted Domain Events or EventBus messages. Domain events must still be recorded from aggregates via recordEvent/commit.
close_reason: Documented that DomainStateMachine outputs are plain returned values, not emitted Domain Events/EventBus messages; aggregate events still go through recordEvent/commit.

title: Clarify DomainStateMachine aggregate documentation
description: The Domain State Machine guide has a good lifecycle example, but the aggregate snippet is ambiguous: it passes this.state as a machine snapshot and then commits only result.snapshot.context. Clarify the intended aggregate shape and output handling before release.
close_reason: Clarified the DomainStateMachine guide with a correct AggregateRoot usage example and explicit output handling.

title: Prepare 2.1.0 release
description: Bump package metadata and changelog for the additive DomainStateMachine release, then verify pack/build/test/docs gates.
close_reason: Prepared 2.1.0 release metadata and verified typecheck, tests, lint, build, docs build, and npm pack dry-run.

title: Upgrade @shirudo/base-error from v5 to v7
description: base-error v6 removed withUserMessage/getUserMessage and the ProblemDetails types + ValidationError.toProblemDetails() method. Migrate: bump peer/dev dep to ^7.1.1; rewrite src/http/problem-details.ts self-contained (build RFC 9457 from publicIssues()); drop default user messages from the three InfrastructureError subclasses (v6 presentation philosophy); update tests and docs. Breaking change to ddd-kit public surface -> 2.0.0.
close_reason: Upgraded @shirudo/base-error 5->7.1.1: self-contained toProblemDetails, dropped removed user-message API, docs+changelog updated. Gates green.

title: feat(testing): Repository Contract Test Suite — RELEASE BLOCKER for any stable/public UoW claim
description:
OCC must be a CONTRACT, not a documented pattern (checklist review 2026-06-11). The kit is ORM-agnostic: the version predicate lives in consumer-repo SQL, so the kit cannot guarantee OCC — it can only ship the contract tests that expose broken repositories. Until this suite exists, the UnitOfWork/OCC feature must not be claimed stable/public.

Deliverable: createRepositoryContractTests(harness) in a new subpath export (proposal: @shirudo/ddd-kit/testing, mirroring the /http opt-in entry), adapter-agnostic — the consumer supplies a harness ({ makeUow or makeRepo(tx), createAggregate, mutate, parallel-capable scope, cleanup }) and the suite runs against THEIR adapter. For SQL adapters the suite must run against a real database (testcontainers), not in-memory; document that requirement in the suite's README/JSDoc.

MANDATORY test (the one that proves OCC): two UoWs load aggregate at version N; A mutates and commits → succeeds; B mutates and commits → throws ConcurrencyConflictError; final persisted state equals A's; outbox contains only A's events.

Further contract tests: insert-vs-update routing on persistedVersion === undefined (mutate-before-first-save must INSERT); update writes aggregate.version and predicates on persistedVersion; zero affected rows → ConcurrencyConflictError (distinguishable, retryable); rollback persists nothing (state, version, outbox); identity map: two getById in one UoW → same instance; deleted aggregate: getById → null, re-save → AggregateDeletedError; changedKeys-scoped child writes still bump root version (partial-write repos); markPersisted only after commit; events cleared after commit, kept on rollback; version-only change still persists (hasChanges contract).

Also ship the in-memory reference repository (with a REAL version predicate, not a stub) as the example consumers copy — clearly framed as example, not proof: only the contract suite against the real adapter proves OCC.

Acceptance: suite exported and documented; kit's own in-memory reference passes it; unit-of-work.md links it as the compliance bar; CHANGELOG notes the contract framing.
notes: Post-ship adversarial review (10 findings, all fixed): (1) CRITICAL dual-bundle class identity — dist/testing.js inlined private copies of ConcurrencyConflictError/AggregateDeletedError, instanceof checks would have failed EVERY compliant published-package adapter; fixed via name-based cause-chain matching (suite no longer imports the error classes at all; robust also across duplicate kit installs). (2) Phantom-event regression from enroll-before-write: catching a save rejection and continuing commits the failed aggregate's events — now a hard rule (guide rule 10 + ConcurrencyConflictError JSDoc: retry = fresh run()). (3) Mandatory test hardened: outbox compared by eventId, optional snapshotState capability for deep-equal state assertion. (4) Capabilities moved to the HARNESS — absent-capability tests are omitted from the array, never green no-ops; no wasted env lifecycles. (5) Stale-delete conflict test (deletesAreVersionChecked) + predicated delete in the reference. (6) IUnitOfWorkRepository canonical interface shipped (efe follow-through); ContractRepository typed over TAgg['id']. (7) teardown failures never mask the contract diagnostic. (8) loadOrFail diagnostics instead of bare TypeErrors. (9) Mutant tests parameterized over the shared harness, pin specific messages, enroll-first. (10) docs: GitHub URL for the reference, sequential-only limitation + adapter-vs-wiring classification documented, InMemoryOutbox rollback caveat.
close_reason: Repository Contract Test Suite shipped as @shirudo/ddd-kit/testing: createRepositoryContractTests(harness), framework-agnostic, 12 contract tests incl. the mandatory two-writer conflict (final state = winner, outbox = winner only), capability-gated version-only/child-collection/cross-instance tests. In-memory reference adapter (real predicate, identity-map read path, enroll-before-write) passes the suite; a mutant test pins that a predicate-less repo FAILS it. Contract refinement: save() enrolls before the row write so the deleted-gate fires as AggregateDeletedError pre-SQL. Guide section 'Proving the contract' as the compliance bar; real-DB requirement for SQL adapters documented. Release gate for the stable claim is satisfied at the kit level.

title: deepOmit pathSensitive mode is exponential on shared-reference DAGs
description: src/utils/array/deep-omit.ts:169 — per-path cloning (correctness fix ddd-kit-ts-yek) deletes completed subtrees from the visited cache; a k-path shared node is re-cloned k times. VERIFIED: 24-level diamond takes 10.5s, depth 30 hangs — DoS for voEqualsExcept/deepEqualExcept callers with ignoreKeyPredicate. Note: output size is semantically forced (per-path clones), so fix = visit budget with descriptive error + documentation. Found by code review of fix branch.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests

title: cloneForVo built-in boundary gaps: symbol keys dropped in Map values, DataCloneError instead of TypeError, identity broken
description: src/value-object/value-object.ts:187 — Map/Set subtrees delegate to structuredClone: (a) symbol keys inside Map values silently dropped (contradicts new docstring); (b) functions there throw raw DataCloneError instead of the documented TypeError; (c) shared-object identity/cycles across the built-in boundary break (preserved on main via whole-graph structuredClone). All VERIFIED. Fix: walk Map/Set entries through cloneForVo. Found by code review of fix branch.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests

title: ValueObject ctor still freezes caller-owned objects (deepOmit shallow Map/Set clone + aliased built-ins)
description: src/value-object/value-object.ts:405 — deepOmit clones Map/Set shallowly (new Map(m) shares entry refs) and aliases Error/ArrayBuffer/Promise; deepFreeze then freezes those shared objects and installs permanent throwing setTime shadows on the CALLER's Date inside a Map. VERIFIED: const d=new Date(); new MyVO({m:new Map([['k',d]])}); d.setTime(5) throws forever after. Found by code review of fix branch.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests

title: deepFreeze dispatches on raw toStringTag without brand verification (spoofed Map crashes vo)
description: src/value-object/value-object.ts:104 — re-introduces the spoofing crash class fixed in deepEqual/deepOmit (ddd-kit-ts-3yh): vo({m:{[Symbol.toStringTag]:'Map'}}) throws TypeError 'obj is not iterable'; spoofed 'Date' gets 16 useless shadow methods. Fix: reuse isBuiltInObject brand checks. Found by code review of fix branch.
close_reason: Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests

title: deepEqualExcept(x, x) returns false when x contains ArrayBuffer or Error
description: src/utils/array/deep-equal-except.ts:30-32 — deepOmit clones ArrayBuffer/Error via structuredClone (deep-omit.ts:181), producing distinct instances; deepEqual then compares unhandled built-ins by reference (deep-equal.ts:198-202). Even comparing the same object with itself yields false — reflexivity broken. Trigger: deepEqualExcept({ buf: new ArrayBuffer(4) }, sameObject, { ignoreKeys: [] }) → false. Same with { e: new Error('oops') }.
close_reason: Fixed: cloneBuiltIn aliases Error/ArrayBuffer/SharedArrayBuffer (all reference-compared types); 5 new tests

title: withCommit: post-commit bus.publish rejection makes committed transaction look like a failure
description: src/app/handler.ts:149-151 — await deps.bus.publish(events) is unguarded. The EventBus contract (ports.ts:46-53) throws after dispatching if any subscriber failed, so a single throwing in-process subscriber makes withCommit reject AFTER the transaction committed and markPersisted ran. Callers treating the rejection as use-case failure will retry, double-executing a committed write; the committed result is swallowed. Contradicts the function's own doc (handler.ts:55-59) framing publish failure as recoverable eventual consistency (outbox still holds the events). Trigger: bus.subscribe('OrderCreated', () => { throw new Error('smtp down') }) — any withCommit emitting OrderCreated rejects despite a successful DB commit.
close_reason: Fixed: post-commit publish failures caught; committed result always returned; observer-only onPublishError hook added; 5 new tests

title: vo()/ValueObject crash on non-empty TypedArray (Object.freeze throws)
description: src/value-object/value-object.ts:42 — deepFreeze calls Object.freeze on every object; freezing an ArrayBuffer view with elements throws per spec. Trigger: vo({ data: new Uint8Array([1,2,3]) }) → TypeError: Cannot freeze array buffer views with elements. deepEqual/deepOmit explicitly support TypedArrays, so VOs are advertised to carry them. Same path affects the ValueObject base-class constructor.
close_reason: Fixed: deepFreeze treats ArrayBuffer views as atomic (pass-through, unfrozen); 6 new tests; documented limitation

title: deepOmit crashes on Promise/WeakMap/WeakSet (structuredClone fallback)
description: src/utils/array/deep-omit.ts:181 + src/utils/array/is-built-in.ts:19-21 — isBuiltInObject classifies [object Promise]/[object WeakMap]/[object WeakSet] as built-ins, but cloneBuiltIn falls through to structuredClone, which cannot clone them → DataCloneError. Trigger: deepOmit({ p: Promise.resolve(1) }, { ignoreKeys: [] }). Transitively breaks deepEqualExcept and voEqualsExcept for any VO containing these types.
close_reason: Fixed: cloneBuiltIn passes Promise/WeakMap/WeakSet through by reference; 7 new tests across deep-omit and deep-equal-except

title: EventBus.publish: synchronously throwing handler escapes Promise.allSettled
description: src/events/event-bus.ts:131 — handlersForType.slice().map((handler) => handler(event)) invokes each handler inside .map(). EventHandler allows sync handlers (Promise<void> | void); a sync throw propagates before Promise.allSettled receives the array. Consequences: peer handlers after the thrower never run, remaining events in the batch are never dispatched, the raw error is thrown instead of being aggregated, and already-started peer promises are orphaned (unhandled rejection, process-fatal in Node 15+). Violates the contract documented in ports.ts:42-53. Fix: map(async (handler) => handler(event)) or try/catch converting sync throws to rejected promises. Trigger: bus.subscribe('A', () => { throw new Error('boom') }); bus.subscribe('A', async () => sideEffect()); await bus.publish([evtA, evtB]) — sideEffect never runs, evtB never dispatched.
close_reason: Fixed: handler invocation wrapped in async closure so sync throws become rejections; covered by two new tests

title: TransactionScope<TCtx>: explicit context generic instead of ambient
close_reason: TransactionScope<TCtx = unknown> added; withCommit threads ctx into fn. Drizzle/Prisma/Mongo tx handles now flow through to Use Cases type-safely. No-context default keeps existing callers compiling. 395 tests passing

title: Drop KitError — DomainError / InfrastructureError extend BaseError directly
description:
KitError is a redundant abstraction layer. Semantically it does the same job as the BaseError marker from @shirudo/base-error (isBaseError(e) predicate). The name is vague ("kit") and the boundary it draws (lib-internal) doesn't actually hold — DomainError is shared between lib and consumer.

Drop KitError. The remaining hierarchy:

  abstract class DomainError<Name>         extends BaseError<Name> {}
  abstract class InfrastructureError<Name> extends BaseError<Name> {}

  class MissingHandlerError       extends BaseError<"MissingHandlerError"> {}
  class AggregateNotFoundError    extends InfrastructureError<"AggregateNotFoundError"> {}
  class ConcurrencyConflictError  extends InfrastructureError<"ConcurrencyConflictError"> {}

Catch pattern at App-Service:
  - instanceof DomainError         → HTTP 400
  - instanceof InfrastructureError → HTTP 404 / 409
  - instanceof MissingHandlerError → re-throw (programming bug)
  - isBaseError(e) (else)          → known structured error, log + 500
  - anything else                  → unexpected, 500

Code impact:
  - src/core/errors.ts — drop KitError class, re-parent DomainError + InfrastructureError + MissingHandlerError directly under BaseError
  - tests — repository.test.ts and event-sourced-aggregate.test.ts have instanceof KitError assertions; switch to isBaseError(e)
  - docs/guide/result-vs-throw.md — rewrite the Error Hierarchy section without KitError
  - CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry explaining the drop

Breaking change for any rc.3 consumer who wrote 'instanceof KitError'.
close_reason: KitError removed; DomainError + InfrastructureError + MissingHandlerError now extend BaseError directly. isBaseError(e) replaces instanceof KitError at the App-Service catch-all. 393 tests passing

title: Integrate @shirudo/base-error as peerDep — KitError extends BaseError
description:
Integrate @shirudo/base-error as the foundation for the kit's error hierarchy. Aligns with the existing @shirudo/result peerDep pattern and gives consumers cause chains, retryable flags, user-facing messages, toJSON serialization, and cross-environment stack traces for free.

Plan:
- Add @shirudo/base-error to peerDependencies + devDependencies (^4)
- KitError<Name>, DomainError<Name>, InfrastructureError<Name> abstract bases now extend BaseError<Name>
- Concrete library errors carry their literal name as the generic:
  - AggregateNotFoundError<"AggregateNotFoundError"> + withUserMessage (user-safe "could not be found")
  - ConcurrencyConflictError<"ConcurrencyConflictError"> + markRetryable() (OCC canonical: reload + retry)
  - MissingHandlerError<"MissingHandlerError"> — no retryable, no user message (it's a programming bug)
- New tests: retryable + cause-chain + userMessage + name literal
- Docs: result-vs-throw.md gets a new "Cause chains and retryable" section; the App-Service try/catch example uses toJSON / isChainRetryable
- CHANGELOG entry in [Unreleased]

Breaking change for any rc.3 consumer who explicitly typed the abstract base names with no generic.
close_reason: KitError + descendants extend BaseError<Name> from @shirudo/base-error (peerDep). ConcurrencyConflictError marked retryable; AggregateNotFoundError + ConcurrencyConflictError ship withUserMessage. Cause chains + toJSON + timestamps + i18n for free. 393 tests passing

title: Split error hierarchy: KitError / DomainError / InfrastructureError
description:
The current hierarchy puts MissingHandlerError, AggregateNotFoundError, and ConcurrencyConflictError all under DomainError. Strict reading:

- MissingHandlerError is a programming/configuration bug (the subclass forgot to register a handler), not a domain-invariant violation. Putting it on DomainError is misleading.
- AggregateNotFoundError and ConcurrencyConflictError are persistence/infrastructure concerns. Vernon argues them onto DomainError because the App-Service catch pattern is the same — but conceptually they are not domain-invariant violations.

Refactor:

  abstract class KitError extends Error {}                     // App-Service catch marker
  abstract class DomainError extends KitError {}               // invariant violations, consumer-derived
  abstract class InfrastructureError extends KitError {}       // persistence + concurrency

  class MissingHandlerError extends KitError {}                // programming bug, NOT a domain error
  class AggregateNotFoundError extends InfrastructureError {}  // was DomainError
  class ConcurrencyConflictError extends InfrastructureError {} // was DomainError

Mapping rule for App-Service:
  - instanceof DomainError          → HTTP 400 / domain violation
  - instanceof InfrastructureError  → HTTP 404 / 409
  - instanceof KitError (else)      → expected library error
  - anything else                   → HTTP 500 / programmer error

Code impact:
  - EventSourcedAggregate.loadFromHistory / restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents return Result<void, DomainError>; they catch DomainError thrown by apply(). MissingHandlerError now propagates (correct — programming bug should crash, not be wrapped in a recoverable Result).
  - IRepository.save throws ConcurrencyConflictError. Now under InfrastructureError. JSDoc updated.
  - getByIdOrFail throws AggregateNotFoundError. Now under InfrastructureError. JSDoc updated.

Docs impact:
  - docs/guide/result-vs-throw.md spells out the OLD hierarchy; rewrite the section for the new one.
  - README in the corresponding section.
  - JSDoc on each error class.

Tests:
  - existing tests that did instanceof DomainError on AggregateNotFoundError / ConcurrencyConflictError need to switch to instanceof InfrastructureError (or KitError).
  - new pinning tests for the three-tier hierarchy: AggregateNotFoundError is an InfrastructureError but NOT a DomainError; MissingHandlerError is a KitError but NOT a DomainError nor an InfrastructureError.

Breaking change for rc.3. Consumers who wrote 'catch DomainError' assuming it covered all three will need to switch to 'catch KitError' (any expected error) or split their handlers.
close_reason: KitError / DomainError / InfrastructureError split shipped. MissingHandlerError no longer on DomainError; AggregateNotFoundError + ConcurrencyConflictError now InfrastructureError. Docs + CHANGELOG updated. 387 tests passing

title: Constrain Identifiable<TId extends Id<string>> for brand-discipline consistency
description:
Architecture review finding (cross-cutting, brand discipline).

src/entity/entity.ts:65 — Identifiable<TId> accepts any TId. IAggregateRoot<TId extends Id<string>> is constrained. This is inconsistent — the whole point of Id<Tag> branding is to prevent silent cross-aggregate id mixing. The collection utilities (findEntityById, removeEntityById, updateEntityById, replaceEntityById, hasEntityId, entityIds) all bleed un-branded TId through.

Fix: tighten to 'export type Identifiable<TId extends Id<string>> = { readonly id: TId }'. Update the collection utility generics accordingly. Potentially breaking for consumers using plain-string ids, but a 1.0 is the right moment for the cleanup. Verify all internal usages still type-check.
close_reason: Identifiable + all entity-helper generics constrained to TId extends Id<string>. Brand discipline now uniform across IAggregateRoot, IEntity, Identifiable. Dead deepEqual import removed

title: Drop functional aggregate API — aggregate()/bump()/AggregateState<S>
description:
Architecture review finding (cross-cutting, library coherence).

src/aggregate/aggregate.ts:36..51 — Two parallel APIs for the same concept: the functional aggregate()/bump()/AggregateState<State> path and the class-based AggregateRoot. The functional path is not used by IRepository, has no event-recording or invariant-enforcement story, and is an outlier relative to the rest of the kit (Entity, ValueObject, EventSourcedAggregate are all class-based or factory-based).

Same shape of problem as the historical Result/Outcome split — two ways to do one thing produces maintenance and learning overhead.

Fix: remove the functional path for 1.0 — drop aggregate(), bump(), AggregateState<S>. Keep AggregateSnapshot, Version, sameVersion (these are still used by the class path). Update the index re-exports, README, and any examples. Breaking change, acceptable for a 1.0 cleanup pass.
close_reason: aggregate()/bump()/AggregateState removed. AggregateRoot / EventSourcedAggregate are the canonical aggregate API; Version, AggregateSnapshot, and sameVersion retained as shared infra. CHANGELOG documents migration

title: Add setClockFactory / resetClockFactory symmetric to event-id factory
description:
Architecture review finding (GB-05, Bernhardt).

src/aggregate/domain-event.ts:201..207 — createDomainEvent has symmetric override mechanics for eventId (global factory + per-call), but occurredAt only has per-call. Deterministic event-sourcing tests (event store replay testing, time-travel debugging, audit reconstruction) are a real use case.

Fix: add setClockFactory(factory: () => Date) and resetClockFactory() symmetric to the EventIdFactory API. No new port — reuse the same module-singleton pattern. Update the createDomainEvent JSDoc and the README accordingly. Test seam parity is the goal.
close_reason: ClockFactory + setClockFactory/resetClockFactory added in domain-event.ts, symmetric to EventIdFactory. Deterministic ES tests + time-travel debugging supported. Per-call options.occurredAt overrides the factory

title: Move Result out of domain layer; throw domain exceptions in aggregates/entities
description:
The library currently mixes two error-handling styles: Result-returns inside Aggregate/Entity/ValueObject code AND Result-returns at the App-Service boundary (Buses/Handlers). Per Vernon/Evans tactical-DDD convention, domain methods enforce invariants by throwing typed domain exceptions — not by returning Result with stringly-typed errors. The current setup also forces every consumer into @shirudo/result even if they use effect/neverthrow/etc., which is anti-DDD coupling.

DECISION: Result stays at the App-Use-Case boundary only. Domain operations throw.

Concretely:

Domain layer (THROW domain exceptions):
- EventSourcedAggregate.apply() / applyUnsafe() — collapse to single throwing `apply()`; throw `InvalidEventError` / `MissingHandlerError` / `EventValidationError`
- EventSourcedAggregate.validateEvent() — return `void` and throw, or return `boolean` with separate reason hook. Currently `Result<true, string>` which is wasted return.
- Entity/ValueObject construction — already throws via `validate()`; keep that path canonical
- guard() — remove. Replace with `assertInvariant(cond, () => new DomainError(...))` helper, or just inline `if (!cond) throw ...` (decide separately whether the helper earns its place)
- voWithValidation — separate decision: keep at App boundary OR remove `voWithValidationUnsafe` (redundant with ValueObject constructor) and keep `voWithValidation` for parse-don't-validate use at the App edge

Infrastructure with EXPECTED recoverable failure (KEEP Result):
- EventSourcedAggregate.loadFromHistory() — event-stream corruption is a normal recovery case
- EventSourcedAggregate.restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents() — same
- (Repository.getById — if 'not found' should be a non-throw path, decide separately)

App boundary (KEEP Result — these are the App-Service contracts):
- CommandBus.execute() / QueryBus.execute()
- CommandHandler<C,R> / QueryHandler<Q,R>
- withCommit()

Required deliverables:
1. Define a base `DomainError` (or `DomainException`) class hierarchy in src/core/. Stack-trace friendly, sprechender Name.
2. Refactor EventSourcedAggregate to throw; collapse `apply`/`applyUnsafe` into one method.
3. Remove `guard()` or replace with `assertInvariant`.
4. Decide voWithValidation fate (likely: keep at App boundary, remove `voWithValidationUnsafe`).
5. Document the rule prominently in README: "Domain methods throw. Result is for the App-Service boundary."
6. Add tests for the new exception types (instanceof checks, message contents).

This supersedes parts of:
- ddd-kit-ts-kdh (guard generic E) — obsolete, guard goes away
- ddd-kit-ts-fsz (apply partial-mutation) — bleibt relevant, Lösung wird "atomar mutieren + werfen" statt "atomar + err"

Blocker for 1.0 GA — must land before API freeze.
notes:
Decisions confirmed:
1. voWithValidation bleibt am App-Rand; voWithValidationUnsafe wird gelöscht (redundant zum ValueObject-Konstruktor der via validate() wirft).
2. Repository: getById behält `Promise<TAgg | null>` (Evans Sammlung-Analogie, TS-Idiom, parallel zu Hibernate/JPA/EF/Marten). Optional ergänzen: `getByIdOrFail(id): Promise<TAgg>` der `AggregateNotFoundError` wirft, für Use Cases die werfen wollen — Caller-Choice-Pattern.
3. DomainError: nur abstrakte Base (z.B. `abstract class DomainError extends Error`), Konsument leitet konkrete Typen ab. Lib selbst definiert nur ihre INTERNEN Fehler (InvalidEventError, MissingHandlerError, AggregateNotFoundError) als konkrete Subklassen — die kann der Konsument fangen oder weiterleiten.
close_reason: DDD-compliance refactor landed: domain layer throws DomainError subclasses; apply()/validateEvent return void; applyUnsafe/guard/voWithValidationUnsafe removed; partial-mutation fixed via atomic commit; getByIdOrFail + AggregateNotFoundError added; Result restricted to App-Service and Infrastructure boundaries

title: Bind EventBus.subscribe handler type to eventType arg
description: ports.ts:58 declares subscribe<T extends Evt>(eventType: Evt["type"], handler: EventHandler<T>). T is unbound from eventType, so bus.subscribe<OrderShipped>("OrderCreated", h) compiles but mistypes the handler. Fix: subscribe<K extends Evt["type"]>(eventType: K, handler: EventHandler<Extract<Evt, {type: K}>>).
close_reason: subscribe<K extends Evt['type']>(eventType: K, handler: EventHandler<Extract<Evt, {type: K}>>); same for once. Existing call sites updated to drop explicit generic

title: Stop leaking internal arrays/state from aggregate + entity getters
description: AggregateRoot.domainEvents getter (aggregate-root.ts:119) returns this._domainEvents directly; (agg.domainEvents as TEvent[]).push(...) mutates internals. Same for EventSourcedAggregate.pendingEvents and Entity.state (entity.ts:132). Return defensive copies or Object.freeze snapshots.
close_reason: Entity state shallow-frozen on assignment; domainEvents/pendingEvents getters return frozen copies; freezeShallow helper exported for use by sibling classes

title: Replace no-op Extract cast with real discriminated dispatch in apply()
description: event-sourced-aggregate.ts:171-174: event as Extract<TEvent, { type: TEvent["type"] }> is a no-op (Extract<U, U["type"]> = U). The per-handler signature Handler<TState, Extract<TEvent,{type:K}>> (L290) is illusory at the call site. A buggy handler index can receive a wrong-typed event. Plumb the actual event type-key K through dispatch so the cast is sound.
close_reason: apply() now type-narrows via generic K; no-op Extract cast removed; replay path goes through private dispatchAndCommit helper

title: Fix loadFromHistory version calc + restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents rollback
description: event-sourced-aggregate.ts:230-237 sets version = history.length, ignoring any prior version (e.g. after a snapshot replay). restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents (L267-283) uses a different formula AND short-circuits mid-replay (L277) without rolling back, leaving partially mutated state. Pick one model and apply consistently with full rollback on mid-stream failure.
close_reason: Version advances additively in loadFromHistory; restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents rolls back state+version on mid-stream DomainError

title: Fix partial-mutation in EventSourcedAggregate.apply() on handler throw
description: event-sourced-aggregate.ts:171-174 mutates _state before pushing the event and before bumping version. If the handler throws, state is partially mutated, no event recorded, no version bump — the aggregate is now inconsistent with its event log. Compute newState = handler(...) in a try, commit only on success.
close_reason: DDD-compliance refactor landed: domain layer throws DomainError subclasses; apply()/validateEvent return void; applyUnsafe/guard/voWithValidationUnsafe removed; partial-mutation fixed via atomic commit; getByIdOrFail + AggregateNotFoundError added; Result restricted to App-Service and Infrastructure boundaries

title: Add eventId/aggregateId/aggregateType to DomainEvent + fix payload doc drift
description: domain-event.ts:42 has causationId but no eventId, no aggregateId, no aggregateType. Idempotent consumers, outbox dispatch, and causation chains cannot be implemented by downstream code. Also: JSDoc L51 says payload is "omitted when P = void", but domain-event.ts:116 actually writes payload: undefined. Either filter or fix the doc.
close_reason: eventId (crypto.randomUUID default + override), aggregateId/aggregateType added; payload JSDoc drift fixed; 15 new tests

title: Improve IRepository + IUnitOfWork API surface
description: IRepository lacks nextId() and exists(id); save returns Promise<void> so callers lose post-save version (breaks optimistic-concurrency UX). RepoProvider<R> in uow.ts is exported but unused (dead code, remove). IUnitOfWork is essentially (fn) => fn() — either rename to TransactionScope or add commit/rollback/registerDirty to make it a real UoW. Decide and ship before 1.0.
close_reason: exists() added; save returns void with markPersisted post-save hook; ConcurrencyConflictError introduced; UnitOfWork renamed to TransactionScope (uow.ts → scope.ts); RepoProvider dead-code removed; nextId intentionally kept out of repo (Vernon's identity-from-user-side)

title: Drop or implement ISpecification — currently phantom
description: src/repo/spec.ts:1-12 declares ISpecification<T> as a pure brand with no methods. IRepository.find(spec) cannot be implemented generically. Either commit to the Specification pattern (add isSatisfiedBy(t:T): boolean plus and/or/not combinators) or remove ISpecification and let each repository define its own filter type. Locking the current shape into 1.0 is a mistake.
close_reason: ISpecification phantom removed; IRepository (CRUD by id) + IQueryableRepository<TFilter> split — each Repository owns its persistence-native filter type (Drizzle SQL / Prisma WhereInput / predicate / …)

title: Decide fate of CommandBus/QueryBus for 1.0 (ship or drop)
description: The docstring on CommandBus (command-bus.ts:80-87) tells users to use "external buses", and there are no middleware/pipeline hooks. The classes are unextensible — you can't add logging/metrics without wrapping every handler. Decide before 1.0 freeze: ship them as the canonical CQRS dispatchers (then add use(middleware) API), or remove them entirely (then drop src/app/*-bus.ts and update README).
close_reason: Decision: ship in 1.0 — in-process dispatcher fits Workers/edge, modular monoliths, tests, CLIs. README now documents the scope and the cross-process escape hatch (use CommandHandler<C,R> with external transport)

title: Move bus.publish out of UoW transactional callback in withCommit
description: handler.ts:35-39 invokes bus.publish inside deps.uow.transactional(...). Classic publish-before-commit footgun: events fire while the DB transaction is still open and may not have committed. Move publish to after the transactional callback returns successfully, or document the constraint that publish must be transactional-safe.
close_reason: bus.publish now runs after uow.transactional resolves; outbox.add stays inside the TX for atomic outbox-pattern. Call-order test asserts tx-start → fn → outbox.add → tx-commit → bus.publish

title: Constrain CommandBus.register / QueryBus.register by TMap
description: command-bus.ts:114 (and the mirror in query-bus.ts) re-introduce fresh generics <C extends Command, R>, so the TMap constraint is bypassed at registration time. A typo or wrong handler is silently accepted; only execute() catches it. Change signature to register<K extends keyof TMap & string>(commandType: K, handler: CommandHandler<Command & {type: K}, TMap[K]>): void.
close_reason: register() now binds key to keyof TMap & string and handler return to TMap[K]; subtype generic preserves narrow command/query types. @ts-expect-error tests prove strict-mode rejection of unknown keys + wrong-typed handlers

title: Remove redundant /utils vs /utils/array subpath export + add sideEffects:false
